headline: Sony’s PlayStation disc factory already repurposed
summary: Sony's Thalgau plant, producing 600,000 discs daily (half for PlayStation), is being repurposed. The shift indicates a move away from physical video game discs. Company president Dietmar Tanzer confirmed the change to focus on microlenses.

headline: Tesla driver charged with manslaughter in fatal crash
summary: Michael Butler faces manslaughter charges after his Tesla killed a woman in her home. Butler claimed he was using Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) at the time. The incident occurred last month in Texas, leading to his arrest.

headline: Meta launches new AI-focused 'Pocket' app
summary: Meta released a new AI app named 'Pocket,' unrelated to Mozilla's former read-it-later app. The new app lets users create and share AI-generated interactive 'gizmos.' Mark Zuckerberg is heavily investing in AI, as seen with this new venture.

headline: Top July 4th sales still available
summary: Many familiar tech deals remain active after Prime Day, reversing the usual sales order. July 4th sales often preview mid-July events. Retailers like Best Buy are still offering significant discounts.

headline: PS5 and 4K Blu-ray usage debated
summary: This article poses a question to readers about PS5's secondary function. It explores whether users utilize their PS5 for media playback beyond gaming. Specifically, it focuses on 4K Blu-ray disc consumption habits.

headline: Neuroscape TCG: A fun cyberpunk card game
summary: IGN provides hands-on impressions of the new Kickstarter success, Neuroscape TCG. The game is described as a 'cyberpunk TCG' and is receiving positive feedback. Early previews suggest it offers an enjoyable gameplay experience.

headline: Moana 3 confirmed by Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson
summary: Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson announced that Moana 3 is currently in development. The beloved Disney franchise is set to continue with another installment. Details about the new film's production are emerging.

headline: Danny McBride details serious G.I. Joe film
summary: Danny McBride confirms his new G.I. Joe movie will not be a comedy. He provided the first details on the upcoming live-action project. Filming for the movie is targeted to begin in 2027.

headline: Mother reacts to rapists' increased sentences
summary: Two teenage boys, previously given non-custodial sentences for rape, received four-year detentions. The victim's mother found the new sentences 'better' but still insufficient. The case highlights ongoing debate about appropriate sentencing for serious crimes.

headline: Strait of Hormuz: Uneasy calm amid tensions
summary: BBC journalists visited the Iranian side of the Strait of Hormuz, finding a mix of seized ships and local life. They were the first international journalists to report from Bandar Abbas. The region exhibits an 'uneasy calm' amidst geopolitical tensions.

headline: Pubs granted extended hours for England-Mexico match
summary: Licensing laws relaxed, allowing pubs to stay open until 5 am for the England-Mexico game. This reverses an earlier government decision not to extend World Cup opening hours further. Football fans can now enjoy the match in pubs for longer.

headline: Burnham hints at tax flexibility for Labour
summary: Andy Burnham suggests potential for 'movement on tax' despite previous pledges. He reaffirms Labour's commitment not to raise VAT, income tax, or national insurance. The likely next PM's comments indicate a nuanced approach to fiscal policy.
